Test Description: Verify GPIO register default values and write/read behavior across the defined address set using provided read/write masks and expected defaults.
Remarks: skip_array used to skip VRRW/unsupported registers; skip_rst_array used to skip default-read checks for selected group registers; soft_reset_chk() is compiled out (#ifdef 0); default read masks DIN LSB out via 0xfffffffe per code comment.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
- CALL chk_rst_val()
  - FOR i = 0 .. CNT-1 (CNT=49):
    - SET addr = addr_array[i]
    - IF skip_rst_array[i] == 1: CONTINUE
    - IF read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: CONTINUE
    - READ data_rd = read_reg(addr)   [READ addr_array[i]]
    - COMPUTE data = (data_rd & 0xfffffffe)
    - IF (data == default_value_array[i]): (optional DEBUG printf) ELSE { def_fail_cnt++ ; printf failure }
- CALL chk_rd_wr()
  - DECLARE chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff,0xaaaaaaaa,0x55555555,0xf5f5f5f5,0xA5A5A5A5,0xffff0000}
  - FOR j = 0 .. 5:
    - SET data_wr = chk_val[j]
    - // Write phase
    - FOR i = 0 .. CNT-1:
      - SET addr = addr_array[i]
      - IF skip_array[i] == 1: CONTINUE
      - IF write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: CONTINUE
      - WRITE write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i]))   [WRITE addr_array[i]]
    - // Read/verify phase
    - FOR i = 0 .. CNT-1:
      - SET addr = addr_array[i]
      - IF skip_array[i] == 1: CONTINUE
      - IF write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: CONTINUE
      - IF read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: CONTINUE
      - READ data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i])   [READ addr_array[i]]
      - COMPUTE wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff)
      - COMPUTE exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]))
      - IF (data_rd == exp_val): (optional DEBUG printf) ELSE { wr_fail_cnt++ ; printf failure }
- IF (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0): finish(1) ELSE finish(0)
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Default value check: For each i where readable and not skipped, PASS if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) & 0xfffffffe) == default_value_array[i]; else def_fail_cnt++ and failure print.
- Write/read check: For each test pattern and each i where R/W enabled and not skipped, PASS if (read_reg(addr_array[i]) & read_mask_array[i]) == ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | ((write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff) & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); else wr_fail_cnt++ and failure print.
- Final PASS when def_fail_cnt==0 and wr_fail_cnt==0 leading to finish(0); FAIL otherwise via finish(1).

Test Description: Validate GPIO falling-edge (negedge) interrupt generation per pin (GPIO[8..39]), ISR handling, raw/group status set/clear, and system interrupt routing.
Remarks: Uses bounded waits with timeout; arm int_pend before generating edge; per-iteration enables only the target bit; conditional build for GPIO0/GPIO1 paths; group raw clear via RAW_STCLR1.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
- INIT test_err = 0
- Optional IRQ enables:
  - IF GPIO0 defined: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(87)
  - IF GPIO1 defined: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
- Enable system register interrupt output:
  - IF GPIO0: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR)   [WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1]
  - IF GPIO1: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR)   [WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1]
- DRIVE pad driver to known state high: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff)
- CONFIGURE per-pin registers for input + negedge + clear any pending:
  - FOR i = 0 .. 31:
    - COMPUTE addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4)
    - WRITE write_reg(addr1, (1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16))   [WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + offset]
    - wait_on(10)
- PER-PIN test loop FOR i = 0 .. 31:
  - SET wr_val = (1u<<i)
  - CLEAR group raw for this bit: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val)   [WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1]
  - ENABLE only this bit: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val)   [WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1]
  - wait_on(10)
  - ARM wait: SET int_pend = 1
  - CREATE falling edge: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff); wait_on(30); WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val)
  - BOUNDED WAIT: timeout = 5000; WHILE (int_pend && timeout--) { wait_on(10) }
  - IF timeout==0: printf timeout error; test_err++
- CALL finish(test_err)

ISR: Default_IRQHandler()
- DECLARE local_wr = (1u<<i); SET int_pend = 0
- RESTORE pad state high: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff)
- READ per-pin rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4))   [READ per-pin]
- CHECK DIN for negedge: IF ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) { test_err++ }
- IF ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0):
  - READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
  - IF ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) { test_err++ }
  - CLEAR per-pin raw while keeping doe=1: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20)|(1<<16))
  - CLEAR group raw bit: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr)
  - VERIFY clear: READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); IF (rdata_grp != 0x0) { test_err++ }
  - IF GPIO0: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(87)
  - IF GPIO1: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(88)
ELSE: test_err++ (raw not set)
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Timeout check: In main loop, FAIL if ISR not observed before timeout (timeout==0); PASS when ISR clears int_pend within timeout.
- DIN check in ISR: FAIL if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) & 0x1) != 0 after negedge; PASS when DIN==0.
- Raw status per-pin: FAIL if ((read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & (1u<<i)) == 0) when rdata bit1 indicates raw set; PASS when the bit is set.
- Group raw clear: After clearing per-pin and group raw, PASS if read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0; FAIL otherwise.
- Sysreg clear: PASS if post-clear read of MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 has 0 in respective GPIOx_INTR bit; FAIL if bit remains set.
- Final PASS when test_err==0 passed to finish(0); any increment yields nonzero to finish().

Test Description: Validate GPIO rising-edge (posedge) interrupts across all pads (GPIO[8..39]) including ISR service, raw/group status handling, and sysreg routing.
Remarks: Uses volatile int_pend for ISR/main synchronization; masks group enable during ISR; clears raw per pin for all j; timeouts bound the wait loop; conditional build for GPIO0/GPIO1.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
- Optional IRQ enables:
  - IF GPIO0: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(87)
  - IF GPIO1: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
- INIT test_err = 0
- Enable sysreg interrupt output:
  - IF GPIO0: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR)
  - IF GPIO1: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR)
- Configure per-pin posedge enable:
  - FOR i = 0 .. 31: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), 0x00020000)
- wait_on(10)
- Set DOE (input mode) via group IOCTRL: WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1..4 = 0x000000FF each
- wait_on(10)
- Enable group interrupt: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
- PER-PIN loop FOR i = 0 .. 31:
  - DRIVE low: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10)
  - ARM: int_pend = 1
  - CREATE rising edge: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xFFFFFFFF)
  - BOUNDED WAIT: timeout=2000; while (int_pend==1 && --timeout>0) { wait_on(10) }
  - IF timeout==0: printf error; test_err++; break
  - DRIVE low again: WRITE write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0x00000000); wait_on(10)
- finish(test_err)

ISR: Default_IRQHandler()
- SET wr_val = (1<<i); SET int_pend = 0
- READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
- MASK group during service: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000)
- IF ((rdata_grp & 0xffffffff) != 0): DEBUG success print ELSE { printf error; test_err++ }
- CLEAR per-pin raw for all j: FOR j=0..31: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j*4), 0x00010000); wait_on(2)
- VERIFY clear: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); IF (rdata_grp == 0x0) PASS ELSE { printf error; test_err++ }
- IF GPIO0: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); READ rdata=read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); IF (rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) != 0 { printf error; test_err++ }
- IF GPIO1: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); READ rdata=read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); IF (rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR) != 0 { printf error; test_err++ }
- RE-ENABLE group: WRITE write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF)
- CLEAR GIC IRQ: IF GPIO0 { GIC_ClearIRQ(87) } ; IF GPIO1 { GIC_ClearIRQ(88) }
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Group interrupt observed: PASS if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & 0xffffffff) != 0 in ISR; FAIL otherwise.
- Group clear verification: PASS if post-clear read of MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 == 0x0; FAIL if nonzero.
- Sysreg clear verification: PASS if (read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1) & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIOx_INTR) == 0 after W1C; FAIL if bit remains set.
- Timeout handling: In main loop, FAIL if int_pend not cleared before timeout; PASS otherwise.
- Final PASS when test_err==0 at finish(); any increment indicates failure.
